content: (b) in paragraph (2)—  (i) for “must” substitute “may” , and  (ii) for “before the start of the relevant year” substitute “at any time before payments are made” ;  (c) in paragraph (3), for “must” substitute “may” ;  (d) in paragraph (4), for “, for any relevant year, must” substitute “may” ;  (e) omit paragraph (5).  (3) In Article 22 (basic payment scheme ceiling)— (a) in paragraph (2)—  (i) at the beginning insert “The Welsh Ministers may determine” , and  (ii) omit the words from “is the amount which” to the end;  (b) omit paragraph (4);  (c) omit paragraph (5).  Additional amendments in the Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) No809/2014 11. —(1) Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) No809/2014 laying down rules for the application of Regulation (EU) No1306/2013 of the European Parliament and of the Council with regard to the integrated administration and control system, rural development measures and cross compliance( 5 ) is amended as follows. (2) Omit Article 22 (allocation or increase of the value of payment entitlements). (3) In Article 23 (recovery of undue payment entitlements), for “revert to the national reserve or regional reserves referred to in Article 30 of Regulation (EU) No1307/2013 ”, in both places it occurs, substitute “be adjusted accordingly” . Huw Irranca-Davies Deputy First Minister and Cabinet Secretary for Climate Change and Rural Affairs, one of the Welsh Ministers 3 December 2025 Regulation 3 SCHEDULE Revocations PART 1 Revocation of legislation governing the basic payment scheme in Wales 1. Subject to the saving specified in Part 2, the instruments listed in column 1 of Table 1 and column 1 of Table 2 (which collectively established the rules for direct payments to farmers under support schemes within the framework of the common agricultural policy) are revoked, in relation to Wales only, to the extent specified in the corresponding entry in column 2 of each of those tables. Table 1 Revocation of specified assimilated law governing the basic payment scheme in Wales Legislation to be revoked Extent of revocation The Direct Payments Regulation. The whole of the Regulation in so far as it applies in relation to Wales Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) No 639/2014 of 11 March 2014 supplementing Regulation (EU) No 1307/2013 of the European Parliament and of the Council establishing rules for direct payments to farmers under support schemes within the framework of the common agricultural policy and amending Annex X to that Regulation( 6 ). The whole of the Regulation in so far as it applies in relation to Wales Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) No 641/2014 of 16 June 2014 laying down rules for the application of Regulation (EU) No 1307/2013 of the European Parliament and of the Council establishing rules for direct payments to farmers under support schemes within the framework of the common agricultural policy( 7 ). The whole of the Regulation in so far as it applies in relation to Wales Table 2 Revocation of specified domestic secondary legislation governing the basic payment scheme in Wales Legislation to be revoked Extent of revocation The Common Agricultural Policy Basic Payment Scheme (Provisional Payment Region Classification) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 2014( 8 ). The whole of the Regulations The Common Agricultural Policy (Amendment) Regulations 2015( 9 ). The whole of the Regulations in so far as they apply in relation to Wales The Common Agricultural Policy Basic Payment and Support Schemes (Wales) Regulations 2015( 10 ). The whole of the Regulations The Common Agricultural Policy (Amendment) (No. 2) (Wales) Regulations 2016( 11 ). The whole of the Regulations The Financing, Management and Monitoring of Direct Payments to Farmers (Amendment) Regulations 2020( 12 ). The whole of the Regulations in so far as they apply in relation to Wales The Rules for Direct Payments to Farmers (Amendment) Regulations 2020( 13 ). The whole of the Regulations in so far as they apply in relation to Wales The Direct Payments to Farmers (Amendment) Regulations 2020( 14 ). The whole of the Regulations in so far as they apply in relation to Wales The Direct Payments Ceilings Regulations 2020( 15 ). The whole of the Regulations in so far as they apply in relation to Wales The Common Agricultural Policy (Miscellaneous Amendments) (Wales) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019( 16 ). The whole of the Regulations The Common Agricultural Policy (Direct Payments to Farmers) (Miscellaneous Amendments) (Wales) Regulations 2020( 17 ). The whole of the Regulations The Direct Payments to Farmers (Crop Diversification Derogation) (Wales) Regulations 2020( 18 ). The whole of the Regulations The Direct Payments to Farmers and Rural Affairs (Miscellaneous Amendments etc.) (Wales) (EU Exit) Regulations 2020( 19 ). The whole of the Regulations The Common Agricultural Policy (Payments to Farmers) (Coronavirus) (Wales) Regulations 2020( 20 ). The whole of the Regulations PART 2 Savings 2. The provisions revoked by Part 1, notwithstanding that revocation, continue to have effect, in relation to Wales only, so far as is necessary for the purposes of finally determining any outstanding rights to, and liabilities arising in respect of applications for, direct payments under the basic payment scheme in Wales made in respect of a claim year ending on or before 31 December 2028. EXPLANATORY NOTE (This note is not part of the Regulations) These Regulations amend assimilated law and other legislation in relation to the Basic Payment Scheme (“ BPS ”) established under Regulation (EU) No1307/2013 (“ the Direct Payments Regulation ”). These Regulations give the Welsh Ministers the power to taper payments under the BPS from 1 January 2026 until the end of 2028 when the BPS will close. These Regulations are made by the Welsh Ministers under powers conferred by section 16(1) of the Agriculture (Wales) Act 2023. Regulation 3 provides for the closure of the BPS on 31 December 2028 and lists legislation to be revoked upon closure of the scheme. Regulation 4 amends the Direct Payments Regulation to allow the Welsh Ministers to taper payments under the BPS until its closure. Regulation 5 amends the Direct Payments Regulation to change the way in which BPS payment entitlements can be transferred. Entitlements cannot be transferred unless each entitlement is transferred with an eligible hectare in the same transaction from the same transferor. Regulation 6 amends assimilated law to prevent young farmers claiming Young Farmer Payment under the Direct Payments Regulation for the first time. Regulations 7, 8 and 9 make provision to close the national and regional reserves, the effect of which being that farmers are unable to obtain entitlements to use for claims under the BPS from those reserves. Regulation 10 and 11 make additional amendments to assimilated law. The Welsh Ministers’ Code of Practice on the carrying out of Regulatory Impact Assessments was considered in relation to these Regulations. As a result, a regulatory impact assessment has been prepared as to the likely costs and benefits of complying with these Regulations. A copy can be obtained from Welsh Government, Cathays Park, Cardiff, CF10 3NQ and is published on www.gov.wales . ( 1 ) 2023 asc 4 . ( 2 ) EUR 2013/1307; Article 5A was inserted by S.I. 2020/1556 (W. 328) ; Article 22 was amended by S.I. 2020/91 and S.I. 2020/1556 (W. 328) ; Article 30 was amended by S.I. 2020/1556 (W. 328) ; Article 31 was amended by S.I. 2020/1556 (W. 328) ; Article 34 was amended by S.I. 2020/91 and S.I. 2020/1556 (W. 328) and S.I. 2025/82 ; there are other amendments that are not relevant to these Regulations. ( 3 ) EUR 2014/639; Article 25 was amended by S.I. 2020/91 ; Article 26 was amended by S.I. 2020/91 and S.I. 2020/1556 ; Article 28 was amended by S.I. 2020/91 ; Article 29 w
